extracted_information: Stripe Tax is a software solution designed to automate global tax compliance, including Sales Tax, VAT, and GST. It helps businesses identify tax obligations, manage registrations, calculate and collect the correct amount of tax worldwide, and enable filings. It's built into Stripe for seamless integration and can be activated with a single click, one line of code, or via API. It also offers the flexibility to automate tax calculation and reporting for payments processed outside of Stripe using its API.

specifications.countries_supported: Over 100 countries and all US states

features.name: Obligation Tracking and Global Registration
features.description: Monitors transactions to identify tax obligations. Manages global registrations, including pre-filling applications to allow submission in new locations in under a minute. Helps understand where businesses need to collect taxes.

features.name: Automated Tax Calculation and Collection
features.description: Determines customer's precise location and calculates/collects the correct tax amount. Validates VAT IDs and ABNs for European and Australian customers, applying reverse charge or zero VAT rate when applicable. Supports hundreds of products and services, keeping tax rules and rates up to date. Displays taxes in a familiar way based on location information (currency, zip code).

features.name: Global Filing and Reporting
features.description: Seamlessly integrates with filing partners to automate global filings, ensuring accuracy and timeliness. Provides detailed reports that meet formatting requirements of each filing location for self-filing, accountant collaboration, or preferred partners. Offers itemized and summarized export of tax transaction data. Generates location-specific sales tax reports including gross sales, exempt sales, taxable amount, and tax due.

features.name: Seamless Integration
features.description: Built directly into Stripe, allowing for quick setup via a single click in the Dashboard, one line of code, or through its API. Compatible with Stripe Checkout, Billing, and Invoicing.

features.name: Support for External Payment Processors
features.description: Allows calculation and reporting of tax on all payments, even those processed outside of Stripe, using the Stripe Tax API.

features.name: Fraud and Error Reduction
features.description: Automates tracking of tax requirements and deadlines, ensuring accurate tax collection and timely filings to reduce errors and potential penalties.

statistics.digital_goods_taxation: Over 100 countries are taxing digital goods, with more planning to impose taxes.
statistics.penalties_on_uncollected_sales_tax_us: Can easily exceed 30% of the sales tax due.

saas_taxability_us_states.Other_states: Only taxable in some states, sometimes only B2B or B2C SaaS is taxed.

statistics.online_businesses_compliance_barrier: 72% of online businesses see compliance as a barrier to international growth.
statistics.hmrc_vat_registration_rejection_rate_202: About 17% of businesses' VAT registration requests rejected.

country_coverage_details: Remote sellers only countries: Albania, Angola, Armenia, Aruba, Azerbaijan, Bahamas, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Barbados, Belarus, Benin, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Burkina Faso, Cambodia, Cameroon, Cape Verde, Chile, Colombia, Democratic Republic of Congo, Costa Rica, Ecuador, Egypt, Ethiopia, Georgia, Guinea, Iceland, India, Indonesia, Kazakhstan, Kenya, Kyrgyzstan, Laos, Liechtenstein, Malaysia, Mauritania, Mexico, Moldova, Montenegro, Morocco, Nepal, Nigeria, North Macedonia, Oman, Peru, Philippines, Russia, Saudi Arabia, Senegal, Serbia, South Africa, South Korea, Suriname, Tajikistan, Tanzania, Thailand, Türkiye, Uganda, Ukraine, Uruguay, Uzbekistan, Vietnam, Zambia, Zimbabwe.

Full support countries: Australia, Austria, Belgium, Bulgaria, Canada, Croatia, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hong Kong, Hungary, Ireland, Italy, Japan, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, New Zealand, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Puerto Rico, Romania, Singapore, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, United Arab Emirates, United Kingdom, United States.

international_tax_rules_examples.EU_digital_goods_sales: Non-EU businesses selling digital goods and services into the EU must collect taxes from their very first sale; generally need two pieces of non-conflicting evidence to validate customer’s location.
international_tax_rules_examples.B2B_saas_vat_requirements: Malaysia, Mexico, and Indonesia require foreign businesses to charge VAT on B2B SaaS transactions.
international_tax_rules_examples.tax_representative_requirement: Foreign businesses must appoint a tax representative for VAT registration in Mexico and Switzerland.
international_tax_rules_examples.canada_tax_registrations: Remote sellers marketing to Canadian customers may need up to five tax registrations.
